text: Delivered dense left eigenvectors (Eigen.eigNonsymmetric Left/LeftAndRight): conjugated rows of V^-1 via real 2nx2n embedding on existing LU — exact-by-construction pairing (no eigenvalue matching), no kernel changes, pure real arithmetic. Convention: wH A = lambda wH, unit 2-norm, NOT biorthonormal, real-Schur pair packing in the already-plumbed leftVectorsPacked. Adversarial review verified the conjugation sign (complex-pair tests pin it), embedding algebra, shared-scale pair normalization, and selection lockstep — then found a CONFIRMED spec violation: the documented defective->Left(SingularMatrix) never fired (LU only flags exact-zero pivots; Jordan blocks returned Right with duplicated/garbage vectors at allConverged=true). Fixed with a calibrated two-part guard (pivot ratio 128*eps*2n — defective inputs sit at ~1e-16+, diagonalizable at 1e-8+, 8 orders of separation — plus 1e-2*||A|| residual backstop); near-defective distinct-eigenvalue inputs correctly ACCEPTED with honest residuals; Right-only on defective matrices still succeeds (guard fires only when left vectors requested). LAPACK-divergence note documented. Iterative path still rejects (documented; two-sided Krylov out of scope — remains seam S4 in the backend-boundary doc). Parity doc §2 row updated to shipped(dense). Verified: coreJVM 294/294, coreJS 291/291, laws 29/29 both, parity 29/29. Committed.
